title: Runway ML Cheat Sheet
description: Quick reference for using Runway ML for image generation tasks.

commands.description: Creates an image based on a text description you provide.
commands.scenario: When you need a visual representation of an idea or concept.

commands.description: Applies AI-powered edits to an existing image.
commands.scenario: When you want to enhance or modify an image easily.

commands.description: Compiles a series of images into a video format.
commands.scenario: When you want to create a slideshow or video presentation.

commands.description: Deletes the background of an image, leaving the main subject.
commands.scenario: When you need a transparent background for an image.

commands.description: Increases the resolution of an image for better quality.
commands.scenario: When you have a low-resolution image that needs to be clearer.

commands.command: Apply artistic style to an image
commands.description: Transforms an image to mimic a specific art style.
commands.scenario: When you want to give an image a unique artistic flair.
When you need a 3D representation of a design or concept.

commands.description: Adds motion to a static image, creating an animation.
commands.scenario: When you want to bring a still image to life with movement.

commands.description: Creates a highly realistic image from a description or concept.
commands.scenario: When you need a realistic image for a project or presentation.

commands.description: Work with others on a shared Runway ML project.
commands.scenario: When you need to collaborate with team members remotely.

commands.description: Saves your project in different file formats for sharing or use.
commands.scenario: When you need to export your work for different platforms or uses.

commands.description: Applies existing models to perform specific tasks without training.
commands.scenario: When you want to use AI capabilities without creating models from scratch.

commands.command: Use Motion Brush for localized animation
commands.description: Instead of animating the whole image, use the Motion Brush tool to paint specific areas (like water or hair) to control exactly where movement occurs.
Use advanced settings to simulate specific cinematography moves like 'Pan', 'Tilt', 'Zoom', or 'Roll' to direct the virtual camera lens.
Use the negative prompt field to explicitly list elements you want to exclude from the generation (e.g., 'blurry, distorted, extra limbs') to refine quality.
Copy the 'Seed' number of a generated image to reuse the exact same noise pattern, allowing you to tweak prompts without changing the entire composition.
Upload a structural image and a style image simultaneously to guide the AI on both the layout and the aesthetic texture of the output.
